name: Lemon Slice Muffins
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Sugar, Or More, If Needed
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Grease standard size muffin tins.
Finely grate the zest from the lemons, removing only the bright yellow portion of the peel. Set aside the lemons.
Combine the zest, water, and 1/4 cup sugar in a small saucepan. Stir over medium heat for about 2 minutes, just until the sugar dissolves. Add the butter and stir until melted, about 1 minute longer. Set aside.
Remove and discard all the remaining peel from the lemons. Cut the lemons crosswise into slices about 1/4 inch thick; discard any seeds. You will need about 12 nicely shaped slices.
Add about 1 teaspoon of the remaining sugar to the bottom of each prepared muffin cup, then place a lemon slice on top.
In a medium bowl, stir together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a larger bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and reserved lemon mixture until smooth. Add the combined dry ingredients and stir just until blended. Spoon into the tins, filling about 3/4 full.
Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until a toothpick comes out clean. Immediately invert the tins; the muffins should fall out. If they resist, gently nudge them with a knife.
